attack_surface.exposure: PLang/App/modules/signing/{sign,verify}.cs and code/{Ed25519,KeyPair,IKey,ISigning,IIdentity}.cs — the cryptographic trust-boundary for inbound Data.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Verify() pipeline: type-check → timeout → expiry → nonce-replay → contracts → headers → hash → EdDSA byte verify.
mitigations: EdDSA verification untouched on this branch.
Expiry still enforced after ExpiresInMs (int) → Expires (TimeSpan, ISO 8601) refactor.
Verified property remains read-only; only the verify pipeline can stamp it.

attack_surface.exposure: PLang/App/Channels/Serializers/Plang/{this,Data}.cs + Filters/Sensitive.cs — transport boundary for cross-channel data.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Sensitive.Strip filters [Sensitive] members before write; Plang.Data round-trips signed envelopes.
mitigations: Filter logic relocated by namespace only (SensitivePropertyFilter → Filters.Sensitive).
Envelope JsonOptions changed from static to instance scoping but remain read-only after construction.

attack_surface.trust_boundary: LLM-emitted strings parsed into JsonNode for variable resolution.
mitigations: Uses System.Text.Json with no custom converters that resolve types from string names — no polymorphic gadget surface.
EmptyStringToNullEnumConverter<T> constrained at factory time to enum types only.
FixJsonStringValues escapes raw control chars defensively before re-parse; bounded by input length, no recursion.

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Maps user-supplied PLang type names / MIME strings → CLR types used by action handler dispatch.
mitigations: Resolution order preserved: primitives → domain registry → MIME → null.
Domain registry still keyed via concurrent dict with init lock.
Generic depth guard MaxGenericDepth = 20 retained (was already in place).
No reflection-by-name path that accepts a fully-qualified type string from untrusted input was added.

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Settings persistence — queries are parameterised; no string concatenation into SQL.
mitigations: Pure rename; query bodies and binding remain identical.

attack_surface.trust_boundary: Reserved variable name list (!Goal, !Step, !Identity, ...) prevents user-defined variables from shadowing internals.
mitigations: List relocated, not narrowed. New IsReserved() helper centralises checks.

attack_surface.exposure: PLang/App/CallStack/Call/{Position,this}.cs — restored execution state after async resume.
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Frame Position payload recovered from snapshot; influences which step executes next.
mitigations: RestoredFrame → Call/Position rename (stage 23). New ExecuteAsync wraps handler invocation with error-stamping and cancellation containment but does not loosen frame validation.

findings.vector: Registry.Assemblies remains a public mutable collection (codeanalyzer v3-1, kept open as latent surface).
findings.preconditions: Attacker would need in-process code already running with ability to mutate App-graph fields.
findings.impact: Low. By the time an attacker can reach Registry.Assemblies, they already have the app process; this is hygiene, not a barrier.
findings.exploit_sketch: Not exploitable from external input on this branch.
findings.proposed_fix: Wrap behind IReadOnlyCollection on the public surface; mutation through Add(...). Scheduled as cleanup follow-up.

findings.vector: Console.Out.Write / Console.OpenStandardOutput residual writes (codeanalyzer v3-2, v3-3) bypass Channels.
findings.preconditions: User runs the affected test/report path; output appears on stdout instead of the wired output channel.
findings.impact: Diagnostic output may leak past channel filters that strip sensitive data on the proper Output stream. Test harness only at present.
findings.exploit_sketch: Not an external attack — internal hygiene.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace the two remaining writes with Channels Output write (existing convention).

summary: PASS. The runtime2-cleanup branch is a structural OBP refactor across 107 commits. No critical or high severity findings. Crypto, sensitive-data filtering, type resolution, settings persistence, reserved-name guards, and restored-frame trust all behave identically post-refactor — verified by line-level diff against origin/runtime2. The two carry-over codeanalyzer v3 items are quality follow-ups, not security gaps. No blockers to merge.
